page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on external storage solutions and delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced vector extensions, including AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI, which assist in vector calculations and cryptography. In stress-ng testing, the server achieved a single-core CPU performance score of 1435.0 and a multi-core score of 15316.01. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show a read-only small block cached speed of 853.47 GB/sec, which drops to 87.94 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while large block write speeds reach 37.05 GB/sec.

For application-specific workloads, the instance demonstrates solid capabilities in data processing and web serving. It achieves a static web serving throughput of 12.43 GB/sec and handles 15.25 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. In language model inference tests, the server processes 935.61 tokens per second for a small 135M model, though performance drops to a weak 23.1 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage, making it suitable for web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 3.1 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via KVM, it provides 32.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15316.01 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.25 million operations per second. The CPU supports AVX-512 and AVX-512 VNNI instructions, enhancing vector and cryptographic operations. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-intensive workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without high memory overhead, making it well-suited for web serving, database operations, and moderate data processing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering reliable performance for web serving and database tasks.
